title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides a flexible ecosystem supporting multiple interfaces including desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK for autonomous agent-based development workflows.
writeup: The video compares the surfaces of Google Antigravity, an agent-first software development platform for building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents, and lays out when to reach for each: the 2.0 desktop app, the IDE, the CLI, and the SDK. It matters because Antigravity is positioned as a flexible ecosystem for autonomous agent-based development, and surface choice shapes how agents get orchestrated across a workflow. The takeaway is a mapping of the four interfaces to their fit for AI-assisted development and agent orchestration tasks.
